name: Community Legal Services Of Mid-Florida, Inc. - Palatka Office
description_short: Community Legal Services provides no-cost legal assistance to low-income Floridians across family, housing, financial, and consumer matters through clinics and direct representation.
description_long: Community Legal Services of Mid-Florida has operated for 60 years as a non-profit organization delivering free legal support to underserved populations across Florida. The organization serves multiple counties including Volusia and Orange, with office locations in Daytona Beach and Palatka, offering comprehensive legal guidance without cost to eligible clients. Their service areas span critical life domains: family law and children's issues, housing and fair housing disputes, financial matters, SNAP food assistance navigation, disaster relief support, divorce proceedings, small business formation, violence protection, and rights for older adults and students. The organization distinguishes itself through specialized legal clinics addressing specific community needs—such as their "Second Chance" sealing and expungement clinic for criminal record relief—rather than generic advice. Unlike for-profit legal service providers, CLS operates on a mission-driven model and has built institutional experience serving vulnerable populations over six decades. The primary limitation is eligibility restrictions based on income and geographic service areas; not all Florida residents or income levels qualify for assistance.
pros: Completely free legal services with no hidden fees or cost barriers
60 years of established experience serving low-income Florida communities
Specialized clinics addressing specific needs like criminal record expungement and family law
Multiple service areas including housing, family law, financial matters, and consumer protection
No-cost legal advice clinics available regularly across multiple counties
Serves diverse vulnerable populations including older adults, children, and disaster victims
Covers critical needs like SNAP benefits navigation and violence protection orders
cons: Limited to specific income-eligible populations; not universally available to all consumers
Geographically limited to Mid-Florida region (Volusia, Orange, Putnam counties based on clinic locations)
Clinic-based model may involve waiting periods or limited availability compared to on-demand services
No indication of 24/7 availability; services appear limited to scheduled clinic hours
Eligibility requirements not detailed on website, creating uncertainty about qualification
best_for: Low-income families needing divorce, custody, or child support assistance
Renters facing eviction, fair housing violations, or landlord disputes
Individuals seeking criminal record expungement or sealing after conviction
Older adults protecting assets, establishing powers of attorney, or addressing elder abuse
diagnosis: Best for low-income Florida residents in Volusia, Orange, and surrounding counties who need free legal help with family, housing, or financial issues and meet eligibility requirements. Main caveat: Services are limited by income qualification, geographic service area, and clinic availability—this is not a substitute for immediate legal emergency services and requires advance planning to access.
services: Housing dispute resolution and fair housing advocacy
SNAP benefits application and appeal assistance
Criminal record sealing and expungement clinics
Small business formation and non-profit legal support
Consumer protection and financial matters counsel
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 605 Saint Johns Ave, Suite 102, Palatka, FL 32177
meta_description: Community Legal Services (Palatka): no-cost legal assistance for low-income Floridians in family law, housing, and financial matters.
